verses.latin_vulgate: Oratio David.   Exaudi, Domine, justitiam meam ;  intende deprecationem meam.  Auribus percipe orationem meam,  non in labiis dolosis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּפִלָּ֗ה לְדָ֫וִ֥ד שִׁמְעָ֤ה יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀ צֶ֗דֶק הַקְשִׁ֥יבָה רִנָּתִ֗י הַאֲזִ֥ינָה תְפִלָּתִ֑י בְּ֝לֹ֗א שִׂפְתֵ֥י מִרְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Hear the right, O Lord, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: De vultu tuo judicium meum prodeat ;  oculi tui videant æquitates.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭לְּפָנֶיךָ מִשְׁפָּטִ֣י יֵצֵ֑א עֵ֝ינֶ֗יךָ תֶּחֱזֶ֥ינָה מֵישָׁרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Probasti cor meum, et visitasti nocte ;  igne me examinasti, et non est inventa in me iniquitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֘חַ֤נְתָּ לִבִּ֨י ׀ פָּ֘קַ֤דְתָּ לַּ֗יְלָה צְרַפְתַּ֥נִי בַל־תִּמְצָ֑א זַ֝מֹּתִ֗י בַּל־יַעֲבׇר־פִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast proved mine heart; thou hast visited me in the night; thou hast tried me, and shalt find nothing; I am purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut non loquatur os meum opera hominum :  propter verba labiorum tuorum, ego custodivi vias duras.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִפְעֻלּ֣וֹת אָ֭דָם בִּדְבַ֣ר שְׂפָתֶ֑יךָ אֲנִ֥י שָׁ֝מַ֗רְתִּי אׇרְח֥וֹת פָּרִֽיץ׃
verses.king_james: Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept me from the paths of the destroyer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perfice gressus meos in semitis tuis,  ut non moveantur vestigia mea.
verses.king_james: Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego clamavi, quoniam exaudisti me, Deus ;  inclina aurem tuam mihi, et exaudi verba mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִֽי־קְרָאתִ֣יךָ כִֽי־תַעֲנֵ֣נִי אֵ֑ל הַֽט־אׇזְנְךָ֥ לִ֝֗י שְׁמַ֣ע אִמְרָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mirifica misericordias tuas,  qui salvos facis sperantes in te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַפְלֵ֣ה חֲ֭סָדֶיךָ מוֹשִׁ֣יעַ חוֹסִ֑ים מִ֝מִּתְקוֹמְמִ֗ים בִּֽימִינֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Shew thy marvellous lovingkindness, O thou that savest by thy right hand them which put their trust in thee from those that rise up against them.

verses.latin_vulgate: A resistentibus dexteræ tuæ custodi me  ut pupillam oculi.  Sub umbra alarum tuarum protege me
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׇׁ֭מְרֵנִי כְּאִישׁ֣וֹן בַּת־עָ֑יִן בְּצֵ֥ל כְּ֝נָפֶ֗יךָ תַּסְתִּירֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,

verses.latin_vulgate: a facie impiorum qui me afflixerunt.  Inimici mei animam meam circumdederunt ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִפְּנֵ֣י רְ֭שָׁעִים ז֣וּ שַׁדּ֑וּנִי אֹיְבַ֥י בְּ֝נֶ֗פֶשׁ יַקִּ֥יפוּ עָלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: From the wicked that oppress me, from my deadly enemies, who compass me about.

verses.latin_vulgate: adipem suum concluserunt :  os eorum locutum est superbiam.
verses.king_james: They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Projicientes me nunc circumdederunt me ;  oculos suos statuerunt declinare in terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֭שֻּׁרֵינוּ עַתָּ֣ה (סבבוני) [סְבָב֑וּנוּ] עֵינֵיהֶ֥ם יָ֝שִׁ֗יתוּ לִנְט֥וֹת בָּאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: They have now compassed us in our steps: they have set their eyes bowing down to the earth;

verses.latin_vulgate: Susceperunt me sicut leo paratus ad prædam,  et sicut catulus leonis habitans in abditis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּמְיֹנ֗וֹ כְּ֭אַרְיֵה יִכְס֣וֹף לִטְרֹ֑ף וְ֝כִכְפִ֗יר יֹשֵׁ֥ב בְּמִסְתָּרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurge, Domine : præveni eum, et supplanta eum :  eripe animam meam ab impio ;  frameam tuam
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קוּמָ֤ה יְהֹוָ֗ה קַדְּמָ֣ה פָ֭נָיו הַכְרִיעֵ֑הוּ פַּלְּטָ֥ה נַ֝פְשִׁ֗י מֵרָשָׁ֥ע חַרְבֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Arise, O Lord, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:

verses.latin_vulgate: ab inimicis manus tuæ.  Domine, a paucis de terra divide eos in vita eorum ;  de absconditis tuis adimpletus est venter eorum.  Saturati sunt filiis,  et dimiserunt reliquias suas parvulis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֥מְתִֽים־יָדְךָ֨ ׀ יְהֹוָ֡ה מִֽמְתִ֬ים מֵחֶ֗לֶד חֶלְקָ֥ם בַּֽחַיִּים֮ (וצפינך) [וּֽצְפוּנְךָ֮] תְּמַלֵּ֢א בִ֫טְנָ֥ם יִשְׂבְּע֥וּ בָנִ֑ים וְהִנִּ֥יחוּ יִ֝תְרָ֗ם לְעוֹלְלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: From men which are thy hand, O Lord, from men of the world, which have their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: they are full of children, and leave the rest of their substance to their babes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem in justitia apparebo conspectui tuo ;  satiabor cum apparuerit gloria tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֗י בְּ֭צֶדֶק אֶחֱזֶ֣ה פָנֶ֑יךָ אֶשְׂבְּעָ֥ה בְ֝הָקִ֗יץ תְּמוּנָתֶֽךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness: I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.
